question: What is the primary purpose of the procedure described in the context of Control Plane CIoT 5GS Optimization?

options.A: To establish a new RRC connection between the UE and ng-eNB.
options.B: To terminate an existing RRC connection between the UE and ng-eNB.
options.C: To modify the AS security context stored in the UE and ng-eNB.
options.D: To allow the ng-eNB to suspend an RRC connection for later resumption by the UE.

_dataset_notice.attribution: Dr Gagan Raj Gupta, 2024, Telecom Benchmark Dataset

question: What happens when a Network Slice instance is congested or no longer available, and the AMF has subscribed to receive notifications from the NSSF?

options.A: The AMF will continue to use the same Network Slice instance for all PDU Sessions associated with the S-NSSAI.
options.B: The SMF will automatically select a new Network Slice instance for the given S-NSSAI without any intervention from the AMF.
options.C: The AMF may delete the old NSI ID corresponding to the Network Slice instance that is no longer available, and inform the SMF to release the PDU Session(s) associated with it, triggering the UE to establish new PDU session(s).
options.D: The NSSF will select a new Network Slice instance for the given S-NSSAI and notify the AMF to use it for all future PDU Sessions.

question: What is the primary purpose of the NAS signalling connection established between a UE and the AMF over N1 in the context of connection management?

options.A: To enable user plane data transfer between the UE and the core network.
options.B: To facilitate NAS signalling exchange between the UE and the core network.
options.C: To manage the AN signalling connection between the UE and the AN.
options.D: To establish a direct connection between the UE and the UPF.

question: What is the rule regarding transmission of a 5G authentication vector to an EPS core network entity?

options.A: It is permitted if the SUPI has the form of an IMSI.
options.B: It is permitted only in mobility scenarios.
options.C: It is not permitted, but unused EPS authentication vectors can be transmitted to a 5G core network entity.
options.D: It is not permitted under any circumstances.

question: What is the condition for a UE to be considered "in session" for E-RABs with bursty flow?

options.A: If any E-RAB data on a Data Radio Bearer (UL or DL) has been transferred during the last 50 ms.
options.B: If any E-RAB data on a Data Radio Bearer (UL or DL) has been transferred during the last 1000 ms.
options.C: If the UE is in idle mode for more than 10 seconds.
options.D: If any E-RAB data on a Data Radio Bearer (UL or DL) has been transferred during the last 100 ms.

question: What happens to the stored list of equivalent PLMNs when the Mobile Station (MS) is switched off?

options.A: The list is deleted.
options.B: The list remains stored and is not deleted.
options.C: The list is replaced with a new list downloaded from the network.
options.D: The list is updated with new entries.

question: Why is it important to differentiate between cell unavailability caused by Energy Saving and other network failures in E-UTRAN performance management?

options.A: To optimize energy consumption without impacting network performance.
options.B: To ensure accurate billing for energy consumption.
options.C: To distinguish Energy Saving-related failures from other network issues, allowing for separate counting and deduction of cell downtime due to Energy Savings from total cell outage.
options.D: To prioritize energy-saving features over other network management functions.

question: What mechanism does an IPX node use to integrity protect the modifiedDataToIntegrityProtect object when modifying attributes of the original HTTP message?

options.A: Symmetric encryption with a shared secret key
options.B: Digital certificates with public key infrastructure (PKI)
options.C: Hash-based message authentication code (HMAC)
options.D: Asymmetric methods with private/public key pairs using JSON Web Signature (JWS)

question: What should a GTP-C entity do when it decides not to process an incoming request message due to overload, if possible?

options.A: Drop the request message without sending any response.
options.B: Send a reject response message indicating temporary unavailability of resources and include Overload Control Information.
options.C: Process the request message partially to avoid dropping it completely.
options.D: Ignore the overload condition and continue processing incoming messages.

question: Which entity determines whether an NSAC admission request for a roaming UE is accepted or rejected in the HPLMN NSAC Admission Mode?

options.A: AMF in VPLMN
options.B: SMF in VPLMN
options.C: Primary NSACF or central NSACF in VPLMN
options.D: Primary NSACF or central NSACF in HPLMN

question: What triggers the counting of all E-RABs for a UE in the measurement of the number of E-RABs attempted to establish for incoming HOs?

options.A: Receipt by the eNB of a X2AP HANDOVER REQUEST or S1AP HANDOVER REQUEST message, or transmission by the eNB of the RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to the UE triggering the intra-eNB handover.
options.B: Transmission by the eNB of the RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to the UE triggering the inter-eNB handover.
options.C: Receipt by the eNB of a X2AP HANDOVER REQUEST or S1AP HANDOVER REQUEST message, but not transmission by the eNB of the RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to the UE triggering the intra-eNB handover.
options.D: Transmission by the eNB of the RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to the UE triggering the inter-RAT handover.

question: When does a UE apply the new multicast MCCH information after receiving a change notification in RRC_INACTIVE state?

options.A: Immediately after receiving the change notification
options.B: Starting from the same slot when it receives the change notification
options.C: After a random access procedure is completed
options.D: Only after the current modification period ends

question: What happens to the UE's RRC connection when it is in the RRC_INACTIVE state, and how can it be resumed?

options.A: The UE's RRC connection is terminated, and a new connection must be established.
options.B: The UE's RRC connection is suspended, but the security context is lost.
options.C: The UE's RRC connection is active, but with reduced capabilities.
options.D: The UE's RRC connection is suspended, but the UE and gNB/ng-eNB maintain the UE 5G AS security context, which can be reactivated when transitioning to RRC_CONNECTED state.

question: What is the primary purpose of the Service Request procedure initiated by a UE in CM-IDLE state or the 5GC?

options.A: To deactivate a User Plane connection for an established PDU Session.
options.B: To request the establishment of a secure connection to an AMF.
options.C: To release the connection to an SMF.
options.D: To modify the QoS parameters of an existing PDU Session.

question: When can the network send an MM INFORMATION message to the mobile station during an RR connection?

options.A: Only once, at the beginning of the RR connection.
options.B: Only in response to a specific request from the mobile station.
options.C: At any time, including immediately after the AUTHENTICATION REQUEST message, without adding delay or interrupting CM layer transactions.
options.D: Never, as it is not allowed during an RR connection.

question: Which entity performs the role of EAP Authenticator in a Home Routed deployment scenario?

options.A: V-SMF
options.B: H-SMF and V-SMF jointly
options.C: H-SMF
options.D: AMF

question: How does the AMF determine the UE presence in an Area of Interest when the UE is in CM-CONNECTED state and NG-RAN location reporting has been requested?

options.A: The AMF determines the UE presence based solely on the reported value from the NG-RAN.
options.B: The AMF considers additional ULI provided by the NG-RAN node only if the UE is served by a MBSR.
options.C: The AMF takes into account both the reported value from the NG-RAN and the N2 Notification from NG-RAN, considering various conditions such as RRC_CONNECTED state, User Location Information, and Registration Area.
options.D: The AMF determines the UE presence based solely on the UE's RRC_CONNECTED state.

question: What is the primary purpose of the Packet-Temporary Mobile Station Identity (P-TMSI) reallocation procedure in a GPRS network?

options.A: To provide identity confidentiality and protect users against being identified and located by an intruder.
options.B: To optimize routing area updates and reduce signaling overhead.
options.C: To enable seamless handovers between different radio access technologies.
options.D: To support law enforcement agencies in tracking user locations.

question: What happens to non-emergency PDU Sessions when a UE has established both non-emergency and emergency PDU Sessions, and is subject to Mobility Restrictions or Access Restrictions?

options.A: The AMF indicates to the SMF to perform a local release of all emergency PDU Sessions.
options.B: The UE locally releases all PDU Sessions, including emergency sessions.
options.C: The AMF and the UE ignore the Mobility Restrictions or Access Restrictions for non-emergency services.
options.D: The AMF indicates to the SMF to perform a local release of all non-emergency PDU Sessions.

question: What is the primary purpose of the MBMS session start message sent by the BM-SC to the MBMS GWs?

options.A: To update the list of MBMS control plane nodes.
options.B: To indicate the end of a data stream for an MBMS bearer service.
options.C: To trigger the modification of QoS attributes in the affected MBMS GWs.
options.D: To announce the arrival of data for a MBMS bearer service and establish bearer resources.

question: What is the purpose of the IE NRDC-Parameters in the RRC protocol specification?

options.A: To contain parameters specific to NR SA.
options.B: To provide general parameters applicable to all radio access technologies.
options.C: To contain parameters specific to NR-DC, which are not applicable to NR SA.
options.D: To define the structure of the RRC protocol messages.

question: In a scenario where control plane and user plane separation of PGW as PGW-C and PGW-U is implemented, how does the PGW-U handle Diameter or RADIUS messages between the DN AAA server in the external PDN and the PGW-C when the AAA server is reachable only via the PGW-U?

options.A: The PGW-U drops all the Diameter or RADIUS messages.
options.B: The PGW-U forwards all the Diameter or RADIUS messages to a different node for processing.
options.C: The PGW-U forwards all the Diameter or RADIUS messages between the DN AAA server in the external PDN and the PGW-C according to the interface specifications.
options.D: The PGW-U modifies the Diameter or RADIUS messages before forwarding them.

question: What type of integrity algorithm and ciphering algorithm are used when an unauthenticated UE establishes an IMS Emergency Session with the AMF, given that there is no shared 5G NAS security context between them?

options.A: NULL integrity algorithm NIA0 and NULL ciphering algorithm NEA0
options.B: Standard integrity algorithm NIA1 and standard ciphering algorithm NEA1
options.C: UE-specific integrity algorithm and AMF-specific ciphering algorithm
options.D: Session-based integrity algorithm and session-based ciphering algorithm

question: Where should the authentication algorithm(s) that make use of the subscription credentials be executed?

options.A: Within the UE's operating system
options.B: Within a software application running on the UE
options.C: Within the tamper resistant secure hardware component
options.D: On a remote server

question: What type of errors are covered by specific procedures in the Diameter Base IETF RFC 6733?

options.A: Application errors
options.B: Protocol errors
options.C: Experimental errors
options.D: Function-specific errors

question: What information element should a mobile station supporting multicall include in the CALL CONFIRMED message if it is located in a network not supporting multicall?

options.A: Stream Identifier (SI) with value "no bearer"
options.B: Stream Identifier (SI) with a new value not used by any existing bearers
options.C: Bearer Capability information element
options.D: Stream Identifier (SI) with value 1

question: What is the primary purpose of the Redirecting party subaddress in mobile radio interface Layer 3 specification?

options.A: To identify a specific redirecting server
options.B: To associate a subaddress with the redirecting party for call redirection purposes
options.C: To authenticate the redirecting party's identity
options.D: To specify the redirecting party's network address

question: What action is the UE allowed to take when it receives a paging message during the UPDATE-NEEDED state?

options.A: The UE can send user data.
options.B: The UE can send signalling information as a response to the paging message.
options.C: The UE can only perform cell selection/reselection.
options.D: The UE cannot take any action until it enters a new substate.

question: What is the purpose of the PCRF providing QoS parameters, including a reserved ARP value, when establishing a PDN connection towards the RLOS APN with a PDN GW?

options.A: To prioritize regular PDN connections over RLOS connections in terms of admission control.
options.B: To ensure that RLOS PDN connections are used only for Restricted Local Operator Services and have higher priority than regular PDN connections.
options.C: To reject IMS sessions established via the RLOS PDN connection if the AF does not provide an RLOS indication to the PCRF.
options.D: To guarantee QoS for voice services involved in Restricted Local Operator Services.

question: What is the primary purpose of the GUTI reallocation procedure in EPS?

options.A: To update the UE's TAI list and DCN-ID.
options.B: To allocate a new GUTI to a particular UE, optionally providing a new TAI list, DCN-ID, or UE radio capability ID.
options.C: To initiate the attach procedure for a UE in EMM-REGISTERED state.
options.D: To perform implicit reallocation of a GUTI during tracking area updating procedures.

question: What happens to the DRB when the gNB decides to release the corresponding QFI to DRB mapping rule in the process of releasing a QoS flow with explicit NAS signalling?

options.A: The DRB is released immediately after the QFI to DRB mapping rule is removed.
options.B: The DRB remains unchanged and continues to carry other QoS flows.
options.C: The gNB sends an RRCReconfiguration message to UE to release the DRB.
options.D: The DRB is temporarily suspended until all QoS flows are released.

question: What is the restriction on PDU session types that can be transferred to an ePDG connected to EPC?

options.A: Only PDU sessions with PDU session type "IP" can be transferred.
options.B: All PDU session types, including "Ethernet", "Unstructured", and "IP", can be transferred.
options.C: The UE can choose which PDU session types to transfer based on the interworking without N26 interface indicator.
options.D: PDU sessions with PDU session type "Ethernet" or "Unstructured" cannot be transferred.

question: What determines the priority treatment of an IMS-based MPS session in the EPS?

options.A: Only the UE's subscription information
options.B: Only the operator's policy and national/regional regulations
options.C: Only the dynamic PCC based on session authorization information from the AF
options.D: A combination of the UE's MPS IMS subscription information, operator's policy, national/regional regulations, and dynamic PCC based on session authorization information from the AF

question: What action does the Serving Gateway (S-GW) take when it receives incoming data for an IMS terminating session with a higher priority than the ongoing paging?

options.A: The S-GW ignores the incoming data and continues with the ongoing paging.
options.B: The S-GW updates the priority of the ongoing paging to match the incoming data.
options.C: The S-GW sends a cancel request to the MME for the ongoing paging.
options.D: The S-GW triggers a new priority paging towards the Mobility Management Entity (MME).

question: What is the primary purpose of the MNO initiating the network-assisted UAS discovery procedure in the context of a remote inspection mission for power line infrastructure?

options.A: To enable the UTM to track the UAS and facilitate 3GPP connectivity for assisting UAS operation.
options.B: To establish a data connection with low latency and reliable connectivity for transporting UAV commands and receiving UAV responses.
options.C: To record real-time video of the power line infrastructure using the UAV's camera.
options.D: To switch on the UAV controller and UAV to serve the remote inspection mission.

question: What triggers the UE to initiate traffic steering to/from WLAN when E-UTRAN sends an "offload" command?

options.A: The UE's capability and configuration information received from the NAS layer indicating which PDN connections are offloadable.
options.B: The UE's current location and the availability of WLAN networks in the area.
options.C: The UE's battery level and the power consumption of the E-UTRAN network.
options.D: The UE's user preferences and the type of application being used.

question: What action does the UE take when it receives an LTM-Config IE with ltm-ReferenceConfiguration set to setup and the current VarLTM-Config already includes an ltm-ReferenceConfiguration?

options.A: The UE replaces the ltm-ReferenceConfiguration value within VarLTM-Config with the received ltm-ReferenceConfiguration.
options.B: The UE stores the received ltm-ReferenceConfiguration in a new VarLTM-Config.
options.C: The UE removes the existing ltm-ReferenceConfiguration from VarLTM-Config.
options.D: The UE ignores the received ltm-ReferenceConfiguration.

question: What signaling protocol is used to release the user plane between the UE and N3IWF during CN-initiated selective deactivation of UP connection for an established PDU Session associated with non-3GPP Access?

options.A: RRC signalling
options.B: S1AP signalling
options.C: IKEv2 signalling
options.D: Diameter signalling

question: What is the main purpose of the primary authentication and key agreement procedure in the 5G System (5GS)?

options.A: To enable mutual authentication between the UE and the network, and to provide keying material for subsequent security procedures.
options.B: To establish a secure connection between the UE and the AMF for data transfer.
options.C: To authenticate the UE's identity to the network using only the EAP method.
options.D: To provide encryption keys for the UE and the network to use in subsequent communication.

question: What is the primary purpose of implicit overload control mechanisms between GTP-C entities when explicit overload control is not supported or enabled?

options.A: To prioritize messages based on their type and content.
options.B: To reduce the overload at the overloaded node by adjusting the rate of incoming request messages.
options.C: To ensure that all incoming request messages are processed, even if it means increasing the load on the target GTP-C entity.
options.D: To prevent the source GTP-C entity from retransmitting request messages due to network errors or overload at the target node.

question: What action should an NF take upon receiving a notification of user consent revocation for data processing subject to that consent?

options.A: The NF can continue to process the data, but with limited access.
options.B: The NF should ignore the notification and continue processing as usual.
options.C: The NF shall no longer accept any service request for data processing subject to a revoked user consent and may notify other NFs to halt the processing of the data.
options.D: The NF can choose to either delete or quarantine the data, but not temporarily retain it.

question: What happens to the association between the old temporary identity (TMSIo) and the IMSI when the network receives an acknowledgement from the user after a successful allocation of a new temporary identity (TMSIn)?

options.A: The association is deleted, allowing the released temporary identities to be allocated to other users.
options.B: The association remains unchanged, and the old temporary identity continues to be associated with the IMSI.
options.C: The network updates the association between the old temporary identity and the IMSI to reflect the new temporary identity.
options.D: The association is temporarily suspended until the next TMSI reallocation procedure.

question: What happens when a UE receives a CONFIGURATION UPDATE COMMAND message after sending a DEREGISTRATION REQUEST message with the same access type?

options.A: The UE proceeds with both procedures.
options.B: The UE ignores the CONFIGURATION UPDATE COMMAND message and proceeds with the de-registration procedure.
options.C: The UE re-runs the ongoing procedure that triggered the generic UE configuration update procedure.
options.D: The UE sends a new DEREGISTRATION REQUEST message.

question: What action does a mobile station take when it is in the MM IDLE state and NORMAL SERVICE, and timer T3212 expires?

options.A: Perform location updating procedure
options.B: Perform IMSI detach
options.C: Respond to paging
options.D: Perform periodic updating

question: How does the UPF send notification related to QoS monitoring information in the context of Event Exposure using Local NEF?

options.A: The UPF sends the notification over Nupf_EventExposure_Notify service operation according to the configuration from SMF.
options.B: The UPF sends the notification directly to the AF without involving any other network function.
options.C: The UPF sends the notification to the Local NEF, which then forwards it to the AF.
options.D: The UPF does not send notifications related to QoS monitoring information in this context.

question: What type of certificates shall network functions support, according to the security architecture and procedures for 5G System?

options.A: Both server-side and client-side certificates
options.B: Only server-side certificates
options.C: Only client-side certificates
options.D: Neither server-side nor client-side certificates

question: What is the value of the pathloss compensation coefficient for uplink power control when Alpha is set to alpha1?
What happens when a 5G authentication challenge sent by the network is rejected by the UE?

options.A: The UE proceeds with a new 5G authentication challenge.
options.B: The 5G AKA based primary authentication and key agreement procedure is terminated, and no further action is taken.
options.C: The network initiates a security mode control procedure to take the existing partial native 5G NAS security context into use.
options.D: The UE deletes all valid KAUSF and KSEAF keys.

question: What is the purpose of the Namf_EventExposure_UnSubscribe request in the context of event exposure subscription?

options.A: To subscribe to a new set of Event IDs
options.B: To modify the notification endpoint of an existing subscription
options.C: To request a change in the reporting type for an existing subscription
options.D: To cancel a previous subscription by identifying the Subscription Correlation ID

question: How is an interface instance identified for non-UE associated signalling in case of network sharing with multiple cell IDs broadcast with shared signalling transport?

options.A: By assigning a unique UE ID on F1-C, Xn-C, and X2-C interfaces.
options.B: By including the Interface Instance Indication in the respective message and assigning an appropriate value to it on F1-C only.
options.C: By assigning an appropriate value to the Transaction ID on Xn-C and X2-C interfaces only.
options.D: By assigning an appropriate value to the Transaction ID on F1-C, and including the Interface Instance Indication in the respective message and assigning an appropriate value to it on Xn-C and X2-C.

question: How does a UE determine if the enhanced coverage feature is restricted or not for a specific PLMN?

options.A: The UE assumes Enhanced Coverage is allowed unless explicitly restricted by the network for a PLMN.
options.B: The UE checks the Enhanced Coverage Restricted parameter sent by the MME in the Attach/TAU Accept message, but only if it has previously indicated support for restriction of use of Enhanced Coverage.
options.C: The UE always assumes that enhanced coverage is restricted and requires explicit permission from the network to use it.
options.D: The UE checks the roaming agreement with the subscriber's HPLMN to determine if enhanced coverage is restricted or not.

question: What happens if there is no full native 5G NAS security context available in the Access and Mobility Management Function (AMF) during the registration process?

options.A: The AMF skips primary authentication and uses an existing security context.
options.B: The AMF runs a new primary authentication and a Non-Access Stratum Security Mode Command (NAS SMC) procedure to activate a new 5G NAS security context.
options.C: The UE is redirected to a different network for registration.
options.D: A primary authentication run is required, and the AMF may decide to run a new primary authentication and a NAS SMC procedure after the Registration Request.

question: What is a condition that must be met for a Mobile Station (MS) to attempt call re-establishment after a radio link failure?

options.A: The MS must have at least two active MM connections.
options.B: The MS must be in the MM IDLE state.
options.C: At least one MM connection is active and a lower layer failure has occurred.
options.D: The MS must have performed a location update in the current location area.

question: What is the purpose of the "Time Period the Requested IP Throughput Cannot be Achieved in the Cell" measurement in E-UTRAN performance management?

options.A: To measure the time period during which the UE is in idle mode.
options.B: To evaluate the quality of provided services to end users in observed cells by comparing IP throughput with guaranteed bit rates of GBR services.
options.C: To monitor the number of UEs connected to the cell.
options.D: To measure the time period during which the eNB is experiencing high traffic.

question: What action should a User Equipment (UE) take when it is unable to comply with part of the configuration included in an RRCResume message?

options.A: The UE applies the configuration partially and ignores the rest.
options.B: The UE sends a failure indication to the network and remains in the current state.
options.C: The UE performs actions upon going to RRC_IDLE with release cause 'RRC Resume failure'.
options.D: The UE retries applying the configuration after a certain time period.

question: What action does the Mobile Station (MS) take with regards to the old P-TMSI signature if no new P-TMSI signature is present in the P-TMSI REALLOCATION COMMAND message?

options.A: The MS deletes the old P-TMSI signature.
options.B: The MS keeps the old P-TMSI signature, if available.
options.C: The MS updates the old P-TMSI signature with a new value.
options.D: The MS ignores the old P-TMSI signature.

question: How often should the sender include Load Control Information (LCI) towards a peer in GTP-C messages?

options.A: Only when the new/changed value has already been provided to that peer.
options.B: The sender may include LCI periodically, i.e., include the information during a first period then cease to do so during a second period.
options.C: In every GTP-C message towards the peer, without any exceptions.
options.D: Only in a subset of the applicable GTP-C messages, as decided by the sender.

question: What method is used for authentication between Security Edge Protection Proxy (SEPP) and Service Communication Proxy (SCP) within one Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) if the PLMN uses protection at the transport layer?

options.A: Mutual authentication using NDS/IP
options.B: Authentication provided by the application layer
options.C: Authentication provided by the transport layer protection solution
options.D: Physical security measures

question: What is the purpose of transmitting the X2AP message HANDOVER REQUEST from the source eNB to the target eNB in an inter-eNB handover preparation?

options.A: To initiate a handover procedure between two eNBs.
options.B: To release resources allocated for a UE in the source eNB.
options.C: To update the MME with the new eNB identity.
options.D: To acknowledge receipt of a HANDOVER REQUIRED message.

question: In a Dual Connectivity scenario with E-UTRAN as Master RAN node and NR as Secondary RAN node, how does the target eNodeB determine where to send the Configuration Transfer message?

options.A: Based on the source address in the GTPv2 message.
options.B: Based on the destination address in the GTPv2 message.
options.C: Based on the MME's routing decision via the S10 interface.
options.D: Based on the candidate en-gNB identified by the target eNodeB using the destination address.

question: What information does the NG-RAN use to select a target PLMN/SNPN for future handovers and radio resources?

options.A: The prioritized list of PLMN IDs provided via Mobility Restriction List from AMF.
options.B: The UE's location and available network resources.
options.C: The selected PLMN/SNPN provided by the UE at RRC establishment.
options.D: The broadcasted PLMN/SNPN IDs in a cell.

question: What is the primary purpose of the Client Credentials Assertion (CCA) in the context of NF-NF communication?

options.A: To provide integrity protection on the full service request.
options.B: To enable the NF Service Producer to authenticate towards the NF Service Consumer.
options.C: To allow the NF Service Consumer to authenticate towards the receiving end point by including a signed token in a service request.
options.D: To establish cross-certification process between two PLMNs for roaming cases.

question: What is the name of the SL-SRB that uses the SCCH configuration for unicast of NR sidelink communication, which is used for the sidelink signalling radio bearer of protected PC5-S message except Direct Link Security Mode Complete?

options.A: SL-SRB0
options.B: SL-SRB2
options.C: SL-SRB3
options.D: SL-SRB4

question: What type of error is indicated by the DIAMETER_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES (4121) Experimental-Result-Code value in the context of MBMS Session Start procedure?

options.A: Permanent failure due to invalid request
options.B: Transient failure due to temporary resource shortage
options.C: Authentication failure due to incorrect credentials
options.D: Authorization failure due to insufficient privileges

question: What is the meaning of a CellReselectionPriority value of 0 in the context of cell reselection procedure?

options.A: It indicates the highest priority for the concerned carrier frequency.
options.B: It signifies that the UE should not consider this carrier frequency for cell reselection.
options.C: It implies that the UE will only select this carrier frequency if no other frequencies are available.
options.D: It means lowest priority for the concerned carrier frequency.

question: What happens when a Network Slice instance for an existing PDU Session is required to be changed due to congestion or unavailability?

options.A: The AMF updates the old NSI ID corresponding to the congested or unavailable Network Slice instance.
options.B: The SMF triggers the impacted UE(s) to release the PDU session without establishing a new one.
options.C: The AMF deletes the old NSI ID and informs the SMF to release the PDU session, then the SMF triggers the impacted UE(s) to establish a new PDU session associated with the same S-NSSAI.
options.D: The network changes the related network slice(s) for the UE without involving the AMF or SMF.

question: What security mechanisms are required to protect traffic on the Xn reference point, specifically for the transport of control plane data and user data?

options.A: Only IPsec ESP with IKEv2 certificate-based authentication
options.B: Only DTLS with mutual authentication, integrity protection, replay protection, and confidentiality protection
options.C: Both IPsec ESP with IKEv2 certificate-based authentication and DTLS with mutual authentication, integrity protection, replay protection, and confidentiality protection
options.D: Neither IPsec nor DTLS is required

question: What is the purpose of the Control Plane Relocation Indication procedure when UE and AMF are using Control Plane CIoT 5GS Optimisation for intra-NB-IoT mobility?

options.A: To request the AMF to release the UE's N2 connection after the UE has initiated an RRC Re-Establishment procedure in a new NG-RAN node.
options.B: To initiate the establishment of the UE's N2 connection before the UE initiates an RRC Re-Establishment procedure in a new NG-RAN node.
options.C: To request the AMF to authenticate the UE's re-establishment request and release the UE's N2 connection after the UE has initiated an RRC Re-Establishment procedure in a new NG-RAN node.
options.D: To request the AMF to authenticate the UE's re-establishment request and initiate the establishment of the UE's N2 connection after the UE has initiated an RRC Re-Establishment procedure in a new NG-RAN node.

question: What is the purpose of the NAS transport procedures in the 5G System (5GS), and what types of payloads can it transport between the UE and the AMF?

options.A: The purpose of the NAS transport procedures is to provide a secure connection between the UE and the AMF, and it can only transport SMS messages.
options.B: The purpose of the NAS transport procedures is to manage PDU sessions, and it can only transport 5GSM messages.
options.C: The purpose of the NAS transport procedures is to provide a transport of payload between the UE and the AMF, and it can transport various types of payloads, including 5GSM messages, SMS, LPP messages, SLPP messages, and more.
options.D: The purpose of the NAS transport procedures is to update UE parameters, and it can only transport UE policy containers.

question: What happens when a gNB receives a downlink packet with a new QFI from UPF, but there is no association to any DRB yet in AS?

options.A: The gNB sends the new QoS flow over an existing DRB and updates the AS mapping rules accordingly.
options.B: The gNB establishes a new PDU session for the new QFI before sending the downlink packet.
options.C: The gNB discards the downlink packet as it cannot be associated with any existing DRB.
options.D: The gNB sends a request to UPF to re-route the packet over a different tunnel.

question: What is the primary consideration for an MME to allocate a periodic RAU/TAU timer value to a UE during Attach and TAU procedures?

options.A: The subscribed periodic RAU/TAU timer value received from the HSS, if available.
options.B: The VPLMN operator policy, regardless of other factors.
options.C: The low access priority indication from the UE, if present.
options.D: The Start of Unavailability Period and/or Unavailability Period Duration, if using a RAN that provides discontinuous coverage.

question: What happens to the MAC entity when a UE performs cell-reselection?

options.A: The MAC entity is modified.
options.B: The MAC entity is released.
options.C: The MAC entity is reset.
options.D: The MAC entity remains unchanged.

question: What is the purpose of the IMSI and APN information retrieval procedure used by the RCAF?

options.A: To determine the PCRFs serving UEs on a congested eNodeB or E-UTRAN cell.
options.B: To report RAN user-plane congestion information (RUCI) to the MMEs.
options.C: To retrieve the list of APNs for all active PDN connections in the network.
options.D: To determine the UEs served by a congested eNodeB or E-UTRAN cell and their corresponding APNs.

question: What is the identifier value for the NIA0 Null Integrity Protection algorithm used in 5G NAS and New Radio?
What happens when a UE initiates a de-registration procedure in a cell belonging to an SNPN with a non-globally-unique SNPN identity for which the UE has no valid subscription?

options.A: The network shall initiate the de-registration procedure and send a DEREGISTRATION REQUEST message including the 5GMM cause #74 "Temporarily not authorized for this SNPN".
options.B: The network shall reject the de-registration request and send an error message to the UE.
options.C: The network shall ignore the de-registration request and continue with the current registration state.
options.D: The network shall initiate a new registration procedure for the UE.

question: In which RRC states can a UE receive MBS configuration for broadcast sessions via MCCH?

options.A: Only in RRC_CONNECTED state
options.B: Only in RRC_IDLE and RRC_INACTIVE states
options.C: In RRC_IDLE, RRC_INACTIVE, and RRC_CONNECTED states
options.D: Only when transitioning from RRC_IDLE to RRC_CONNECTED

question: What uniquely identifies a Network Slice Instance within a PLMN or SNPN when multiple instances of the same Network Slice are deployed?

options.A: Network Slice Instance Identifier (NSI ID)
options.B: S-NSSAI
options.C: UE ID
options.D: PLMN ID

question: How long does the UE store the Successful PSCell Addition/Change Report (SPR) if it is not fetched by the network?

options.A: Until the SPR is fetched by the network or for 24 hours after the SPR is recorded.
options.B: Until the SPR is fetched by the network or for 72 hours after the SPR is recorded.
options.C: Until the SPR is fetched by the network or for 48 hours after the SPR is recorded.
options.D: The UE does not store the SPR if it is not fetched by the network.

question: What action is a UE required to take when it is attached for emergency bearer services?

options.A: Perform periodic tracking area updating
options.B: Initiate normal and combined tracking area updating
options.C: Respond to paging messages without any conditions

question: What is the primary purpose of the Nnef_MemberUESelectionAssistance_Notify service operation in the 5G System (5GS)?

options.B: To request additional filtering criteria from the Application Function (AF).

options.D: To indicate the result of an operation execution.

question: What is the purpose of the Namf_Communication_N1N2MessageUnSubscribe service operation in the 5G system?

options.A: To subscribe to a particular N1 message type or a specific N2 information type about the UE.
options.B: To unsubscribe from a particular N1 message type or a specific N2 information type about the UE, stopping notifications to the NF.
options.C: To update the binding for the consumer NF for the requested N1 message type.
options.D: To retrieve the list of subscribed N1 message types or N2 information types for an NF.

question: What is the primary purpose of the security function in EPS that involves authentication of the UE by the network?

options.A: To provide user data and signalling confidentiality.
options.B: To authenticate the network by the UE.
options.C: To provision origin authentication of signalling and user data.
options.D: To guard against unauthorised EPS service usage.

question: What is the primary purpose of the Nnef_AMInfluence_Notify operation in the 5G System?

options.A: To request AM influence data from the AF.

options.C: To provide event information to the AF for policy and charging control.
options.D: To execute an operation based on the AF Transaction Id.

question: What happens to the "forbidden PLMNs for attach in S101 mode" list when the UE is switched off or when the USIM is removed?

options.B: The list remains unchanged.

question: What happens to the stored UE Radio Capability for Paging Information when a UE performs an Attach procedure or a Tracking Area Update procedure for the "first TAU following GERAN/UTRAN/ Attach" or for "UE radio capability update"?

question: What action does the MAC entity take when it receives an SCI on the PSCCH for sidelink communication with a Group Destination ID of interest?

options.B: It determines the set of subframes in which reception of the first transport blocks occur, stores the SCI and associated HARQ information, and delivers them to the Sidelink HARQ Entity.
options.C: It only stores the SCI and associated HARQ information without determining the set of subframes for reception of the first transport blocks.
options.D: It ignores the SCI and does not take any further action.

question: What type of message should be returned in response to a SETUP message containing invalid mandatory information?

question: What happens when a UE in substate 5GMM-DEREGISTERED.eCALL-INACTIVE receives a request from upper layers to establish an eCall over IMS?

options.A: The UE ignores the request and remains in substate 5GMM-DEREGISTERED.eCALL-INACTIVE.
options.B: The UE enters state 5GMM-NULL and powers off.
options.C: The UE initiates 5GMM signalling with the network to establish a non-emergency call.
options.D: The UE enters state 5GMM-DEREGISTERED.ATTEMPTING-REGISTRATION and uses relevant procedures to establish the eCall over IMS at the earliest opportunity.

question: What happens to the NAS signalling connection when a UE provides unavailability information in the attach procedure or tracking area updating procedure?

options.A: The MME maintains the NAS signalling connection until the UE registers for normal service without providing unavailability information.
options.B: The MME releases the NAS signalling connection after the completion of the attach procedure or the tracking area updating procedure in which the UE provided unavailability information.
options.C: The MME suspends the NAS signalling connection until the UE provides updated unavailability information.
options.D: The MME terminates the NAS signalling connection and initiates a new attach procedure.

question: What triggers the Network Slice-Specific Authentication and Authorization procedure for an S-NSSAI requiring authentication and authorization with an AAA Server?

options.A: The AMF determines that Network Slice-Specific Authentication and Authorization is required for an S-NSSAI in the current Allowed NSSAI or Partially Allowed NSSAI during a UE-initiated service request.
options.B: The H-PLMN operator manually initiates the procedure for all UEs with an active subscription to the network slice.
options.C: The AMF determines that Network Slice-Specific Authentication and Authorization is required for an S-NSSAI in the current Allowed NSSAI or Partially Allowed NSSAI during a Registration procedure, or when the AAA Server that authenticated the Network Slice triggers a re-authentication.
options.D: The UE initiates the procedure by sending a specific request to the AMF.

question: What type of authentication procedure may be triggered by the ON-SNPN after successful primary authentication without using DCS?

options.A: Primary authentication using DCS
options.B: Secondary authentication using Default UE credentials
options.C: Certificate-based authentication for primary and secondary authentication with different client certificates
options.D: EAP-TLS authentication for primary authentication only

question: What happens to the application layer measurement configurations when a UE enters RRC_IDLE state?

options.A: All application layer measurement configurations are released.
options.B: Only the configurations explicitly indicated by the gNB as applicable in RRC_IDLE and RRC_INACTIVE states are stored.
options.C: The UE releases all application layer measurement configurations except the ones explicitly indicated by the gNB as applicable in RRC_IDLE and RRC_INACTIVE states.
options.D: All application layer measurement configurations are deleted.

question: What is the condition for retaining the current KgNB during an intra-gNB-CU handover, as indicated by the gNB in the HO Command message?

options.A: The current KgNB can always be retained.
options.B: The current KgNB can only be retained if an unused {NH, NCC} pair is available in the gNB.
options.C: The current KgNB can only be retained if no unused {NH, NCC} pair is available in the gNB.
options.D: The current KgNB cannot be retained during intra-gNB-CU handover.

question: What is the primary purpose of the Secondary Node Addition procedure in E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity?

options.A: To establish a UE context at the SN and provide radio resources from the SN to the UE.
options.B: To update the path during the handover procedure.
options.C: To release radio resources from the MN to the UE.
options.D: To initiate a new call setup.

question: What happens to the new mapped EPS security context if an inter-system change from A/Gb mode or Iu mode to S1 mode in EMM-CONNECTED mode fails?

options.A: The MME and UE will use the new mapped EPS security context for all future handovers to E-UTRAN.
options.B: The MME and UE will update the existing EPS security context with the new mapped one, ensuring seamless handover.
options.C: The MME and UE will ignore the new mapped EPS security context and continue using the old one.
options.D: The MME and UE shall delete the new mapped EPS security context.
